effect: Non-functional allele — splicing defect abolishes enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: One copy: intermediate metabolizer (IM). Two copies: poor metabolizer (PM). PM status leads to no conversion of codeine to morphine (no pain relief) and increased side effects from drugs cleared by CYP2D6.
clinical_significance: Most common CYP2D6 loss-of-function allele in Caucasians (~20% allele frequency). PMs should avoid codeine/tramadol, use alternative antidepressants, and receive tamoxifen alternative.
description: Splicing defect in intron 3 (G>A at position 1846) that creates a premature stop codon. No functional CYP2D6 enzyme produced from this allele.

effect: Decreased function allele — unstable enzyme with reduced activity
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2D6 activity (~50% of normal per allele). Common cause of intermediate metabolizer status in East Asian populations.
clinical_significance: Most common CYP2D6 decreased-function allele in East Asians (~40% allele frequency). Dose adjustments may be needed for CYP2D6 substrates.
description: Pro34Ser substitution causing enzyme instability. Produces a functional but unstable protein with decreased catalytic activity.

effect: Non-functional allele — single base deletion causing frameshift
metabolizer_impact: No enzyme activity from this allele. Contributes to PM status when combined with another non-functional allele.
clinical_significance: Found in ~1-3% of Caucasians. Same clinical implications as *4 for drug metabolism.
description: Single nucleotide deletion in exon 3 causing frameshift and premature termination. No functional protein produced.

effect: Normal function allele — fully active enzyme
metabolizer_impact: Normal metabolizer status. Standard drug metabolism expected.
clinical_significance: Common normal-function allele. Arg296Cys substitution does not affect enzyme activity. Important for distinguishing from non-functional alleles.
description: Arg296Cys (G>A) substitution. Defines the *2 haplotype which has normal enzyme activity. Very common allele across all populations.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced mRNA expression due to splicing defect
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2D6 activity. Contributes to intermediate metabolizer status.
clinical_significance: Common in Middle Eastern and North African populations (~20% allele frequency). Important for pharmacogenomic testing in diverse populations.
description: Intronic variant (2988G>A) causing aberrant splicing and reduced mRNA levels. Produces functional but reduced amounts of enzyme.

effect: Non-functional allele — splicing defect abolishes enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: One copy: intermediate metabolizer. Two copies: poor metabolizer. PM status critically impairs clopidogrel activation, leading to treatment failure and increased cardiovascular events.
clinical_significance: Most common CYP2C19 loss-of-function allele (~15% Caucasian, ~30% East Asian). FDA black box warning on clopidogrel for PMs. Use prasugrel or ticagrelor instead.
description: Aberrant splice site variant (681G>A) in exon 5 creating a premature stop codon. No functional enzyme produced.

effect: Non-functional allele — premature stop codon
metabolizer_impact: No enzyme activity from this allele. Same clinical impact as *2.
clinical_significance: More common in East Asian populations (~5% allele frequency). Rare in Caucasians (<0.5%).
description: Premature stop codon (636G>A, W212X) in exon 4 producing a truncated, non-functional protein.

effect: Increased function allele — enhanced promoter activity
metabolizer_impact: Ultrarapid metabolizer when homozygous or combined with *1. Increased clopidogrel activation (beneficial). Reduced PPI and antidepressant levels (may need higher doses).
clinical_significance: Common in Caucasians (~21% allele frequency) and Africans. Ultrarapid metabolizers may have increased bleeding risk on clopidogrel and subtherapeutic PPI/antidepressant levels.
description: Promoter variant (-806C>T) increasing CYP2C19 transcription and enzyme levels.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced enzyme activity to ~50%
metabolizer_impact: Reduced warfarin clearance. *2 carriers require ~20% lower warfarin dose. Combined with VKORC1 genotype for optimal dosing.
clinical_significance: Common in Caucasians (~13% allele frequency). Rare in East Asians and Africans. Critical for warfarin dose individualization.
description: Arg144Cys substitution reducing enzyme catalytic activity to approximately 50% of normal.

effect: Decreased function allele — significantly reduced enzyme activity to ~10%
metabolizer_impact: Substantially reduced warfarin clearance. *3 carriers require ~33% lower warfarin dose. *3/*3 homozygotes need ~70% dose reduction.
clinical_significance: Found in ~7% of Caucasians. Has the most significant clinical impact on warfarin dosing among CYP2C9 variants. High bleeding risk if not dose-adjusted.
description: Ile359Leu substitution in active site severely reducing catalytic activity to ~10% of wild-type.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2C9 activity. Warfarin dose reduction recommended.
clinical_significance: Rare variant (~1% allele frequency). Most commonly found in African-American populations.
description: Asp360Glu substitution reducing enzyme activity. Important for pharmacogenomics in African-descent populations.

effect: Non-functional allele — splicing defect prevents functional protein production
metabolizer_impact: *3/*3 homozygotes (non-expressors): standard tacrolimus starting dose. *1 carriers (expressors): require 1.5-2x higher tacrolimus doses to achieve target concentrations.
clinical_significance: Very common: ~85-95% of Caucasians are *3/*3 (non-expressors). ~30-50% of Africans express CYP3A5 (*1 carriers). Critical for tacrolimus dosing in transplant patients.
description: Intronic variant (6986A>G) creating a cryptic splice site that produces a truncated non-functional mRNA. The most common cause of CYP3A5 non-expression.

effect: Regulatory variant affecting VKORC1 expression — primary determinant of warfarin sensitivity
metabolizer_impact: T/T genotype: high VKORC1 expression — higher warfarin dose needed (avg ~6mg/day). C/T: intermediate (avg ~4.5mg/day). C/C: low VKORC1 expression — warfarin sensitive (avg ~2.5mg/day).
clinical_significance: Accounts for ~25% of warfarin dose variability. Combined with CYP2C9 genotype and clinical factors, pharmacogenomic dosing reduces adverse events by ~30%.
description: Promoter variant (-1639G>A) affecting VKORC1 transcription. The A allele reduces VKORC1 protein levels, making patients more sensitive to warfarin's anticoagulant effect.

effect: Non-functional allele — exon skipping abolishes DPD activity
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous: ~50% DPD activity. Dose reduction of 50% required for fluoropyrimidines. Homozygous: complete DPD deficiency — fluoropyrimidines are CONTRAINDICATED (potentially lethal).
clinical_significance: Found in ~1-2% of Caucasians. Heterozygous carriers have 50% increased risk of severe/fatal fluoropyrimidine toxicity. MANDATORY pre-treatment testing in EU since 2020.
description: Splice site variant (IVS14+1G>A) causing complete skipping of exon 14 and loss of DPD enzyme activity. Most clinically significant DPYD variant.

effect: Non-functional allele — missense mutation abolishing enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Same clinical impact as *2A. Heterozygous: 50% dose reduction required. Homozygous: fluoropyrimidines contraindicated.
clinical_significance: Rare but high-impact variant. Included in CPIC guideline recommendations for DPYD testing.
description: Ile560Ser substitution (c.1679T>G) that eliminates DPD catalytic activity.

effect: Decreased function variant — reduced DPD enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous: reduced DPD activity. 25-50% dose reduction recommended for fluoropyrimidines.
clinical_significance: Activity score 1.5 (when heterozygous with normal allele). Requires dose modification but less severe than *2A.
description: Asp949Val substitution (c.2846A>T) reducing DPD enzyme activity. One of four key DPYD variants recommended for clinical testing.

effect: Non-functional allele — reduced TPMT protein stability
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous: intermediate methylator — 30-70% dose reduction needed. Homozygous: poor methylator — thiopurines contraindicated or require 90% dose reduction.
clinical_significance: Found in ~1-2% of Caucasians. Critical for preventing life-threatening myelosuppression from thiopurine drugs used in IBD, leukemia, and transplantation.
description: Ala154Thr substitution causing rapid protein degradation via the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway.

effect: Non-functional allele — reduced TPMT protein stability
metabolizer_impact: Same clinical impact as *3B. Heterozygous: intermediate. Homozygous: contraindicated or extreme dose reduction.
clinical_significance: Most common non-functional TPMT allele in East Asian and African populations. Combined with *3B, accounts for ~95% of TPMT-deficient alleles.
description: Tyr240Cys substitution causing protein misfolding and accelerated degradation. Defines the *3C allele; present alone or as part of *3A (with *3B).

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced UGT1A1 expression due to TA repeat expansion
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous (*1/*28): modestly reduced glucuronidation. Homozygous (*28/*28): significantly reduced — 30-70% decreased UGT1A1 activity.
clinical_significance: *28/*28 homozygotes have 9.3x risk of severe neutropenia with irinotecan. Dose reduction recommended. Also causes Gilbert's syndrome (benign unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia).
description: TA repeat polymorphism in UGT1A1 promoter: normal is (TA)6 (*1), *28 is (TA)7. The additional TA repeat reduces transcription efficiency.

effect: Decreased function — reduced ABCG2 transporter activity
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous: ~1.7x increased rosuvastatin exposure. Homozygous: ~2.4x increased exposure. Dose cap recommended.
clinical_significance: Common in East Asians (~30% allele frequency). FDA-recommended rosuvastatin dose cap of 20mg for ABCG2 poor function. Also affects uric acid transport — associated with gout risk.
description: Gln141Lys (c.421C>A) substitution reducing ABCG2 efflux transporter expression and function on cell membranes.

effect: Tag SNP for HLA-B*57:01 — predictor of abacavir hypersensitivity
metabolizer_impact: HLA-B*57:01 positive: CONTRAINDICATED for abacavir. 48% risk of hypersensitivity reaction that can be fatal on rechallenge.
clinical_significance: Mandatory HLA-B*57:01 testing before abacavir prescription. One of the most successful pharmacogenomic implementations. ~6-8% of Caucasians are positive.
description: Tag SNP in strong linkage disequilibrium with HLA-B*57:01. The HLA allele triggers a severe T-cell mediated immune reaction to abacavir.

effect: Tag SNP for HLA-B*15:02 — predictor of carbamazepine-induced SJS/TEN
metabolizer_impact: HLA-B*15:02 positive: CONTRAINDICATED for carbamazepine. Risk of Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS) and toxic epidermal necrolysis (TEN), which can be fatal.
clinical_significance: Prevalent in Southeast Asian populations (~8% in Han Chinese, ~15% in some Thai populations). FDA black box warning on carbamazepine requires testing in at-risk populations.
description: Tag SNP associated with HLA-B*15:02. This HLA allele presents carbamazepine metabolites to T-cells, triggering severe cutaneous adverse reactions.

effect: Variant affecting interferon lambda 3 expression and hepatitis C treatment response
metabolizer_impact: CC genotype: ~80% sustained virological response (SVR) with pegIFN/RBV for HCV genotype 1. CT: ~50% SVR. TT: ~25% SVR.
clinical_significance: Strongest pretreatment predictor of HCV treatment response with interferon-based regimens. Less relevant with direct-acting antivirals (DAAs) which have >95% SVR regardless of genotype.
description: Variant near IL28B/IFNL3 gene affecting innate immune response to HCV. CC genotype associated with higher interferon-stimulated gene expression.

effect: Increased inducibility allele — higher enzyme activity in smokers/exposed individuals
metabolizer_impact: A/A genotype with inducer exposure (smoking, charbroiled food): ultrarapid CYP1A2 activity. May need higher clozapine/olanzapine doses. C allele carriers: normal/slow metabolism.
clinical_significance: Very common variant. Clinical significance mainly in smokers — CYP1A2 ultrarapid metabolizers who smoke may have subtherapeutic clozapine levels. Also affects caffeine metabolism speed.
description: Intronic variant (-163C>A) enhancing CYP1A2 inducibility by AhR ligands (polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in cigarette smoke, charred foods).

effect: Slow acetylator allele — reduced NAT2 enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Slow acetylators: increased risk of isoniazid hepatotoxicity, hydralazine-induced lupus, and drug-induced adverse effects. Fast acetylators may need higher doses.
clinical_significance: NAT2 slow acetylator phenotype affects ~50-70% of Caucasians, ~10-30% of East Asians, ~50-60% of Africans. Critical for isoniazid dosing in TB treatment.
description: Ile114Thr substitution reducing NAT2 protein stability and catalytic activity. One of several SNPs defining NAT2 slow acetylator haplotypes.

effect: Slow acetylator allele — reduced NAT2 enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Contributes to slow acetylator phenotype. Combined with other slow alleles determines acetylation speed.
clinical_significance: One of the major NAT2 slow acetylator alleles globally. Important for isoniazid pharmacokinetics.
description: Arg197Gln substitution (G590A) reducing NAT2 enzyme activity. Defines the *6 haplotype.

effect: Decreased function — reduced hepatic uptake transporter OATP1B1 activity
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous (TC): 4.5x increased risk of simvastatin myopathy. Homozygous (CC): 16.9x increased risk. Dose-dependent effect.
clinical_significance: FDA warning on simvastatin 80mg dose partly based on SLCO1B1 data. CPIC recommends simvastatin avoidance or dose limitation for CC genotype. Consider pravastatin/rosuvastatin as alternatives.
description: Val174Ala (c.521T>C) substitution reducing OATP1B1 transporter activity and hepatic statin uptake, increasing systemic statin exposure.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced enzyme activity due to missense mutation
metabolizer_impact: Reduced glucuronidation capacity. Similar clinical impact to *28 for irinotecan toxicity risk.
clinical_significance: Most common UGT1A1 decreased-function allele in East Asian populations (~13-25% allele frequency). Rarely found in Caucasians. Important for irinotecan dosing in Asian patients.
description: Gly71Arg substitution (c.211G>A) reducing UGT1A1 catalytic activity. Common cause of Gilbert's syndrome in East Asians.

effect: Altered P-glycoprotein expression affecting drug efflux
metabolizer_impact: TT genotype: reduced P-gp expression — increased drug absorption and brain penetration. CC genotype: higher P-gp expression — reduced drug bioavailability.
clinical_significance: Common variant (~50% allele frequency). Clinical impact debated but may affect digoxin levels, immunosuppressant dosing, and opioid brain penetration.
description: Synonymous variant (3435C>T, Ile1145Ile) affecting mRNA stability and P-glycoprotein expression levels. In linkage disequilibrium with other functional ABCB1 variants.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2D6 activity. Contributes to intermediate metabolizer phenotype.
clinical_significance: Common in African populations (~20-34% allele frequency). Rare in Caucasians and East Asians. Important for pharmacogenomics in people of African descent.
description: Thr107Ile substitution reducing enzyme catalytic activity. One of the key alleles for CYP2D6 pharmacogenomics in African-descent populations.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2C9 activity requiring warfarin dose adjustment.
clinical_significance: Most common CYP2C9 decreased-function allele in African Americans (~6% allele frequency). Critical for warfarin dosing equity across populations.
description: Arg150His substitution reducing CYP2C9 activity. Important for pharmacogenomics in African-descent populations.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2C9 activity. Warfarin dose reduction may be needed.
clinical_significance: Found in ~2-4% of Caucasians. Contributes to variability in warfarin dose requirements.
description: Arg335Trp substitution reducing CYP2C9 catalytic activity.

effect: Increased function allele — enhanced enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Increased CYP2B6 activity. Rapid metabolizers may have subtherapeutic efavirenz levels and increased risk of virological failure.
clinical_significance: Relevant for HIV treatment with efavirenz-based regimens. May require dose adjustment or alternative NNRTI.
description: Lys262Arg substitution increasing CYP2B6 catalytic activity.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced enzyme expression
metabolizer_impact: Slow metabolizer phenotype. Increased efavirenz exposure — higher risk of CNS side effects. Lower efavirenz dose (400mg) may be sufficient.
clinical_significance: Common globally: ~25-40% in most populations. WHO now recommends 400mg efavirenz dose which works well for slow metabolizers.
description: Gln172His and splice variant combination reducing CYP2B6 protein expression. The most common CYP2B6 decreased-function allele worldwide.

effect: Altered mu-opioid receptor function affecting opioid response
metabolizer_impact: A/A genotype: standard opioid response. G carriers: may require higher opioid doses for adequate analgesia. Also affects response to alcohol and naltrexone for addiction treatment.
clinical_significance: The G allele (Asn40Asp) is found in ~15% of Caucasians. Associated with reduced opioid potency, altered pain sensitivity, and better naltrexone response for alcohol dependence.
description: Asn40Asp (A118G) variant reducing mu-opioid receptor expression and beta-endorphin binding affinity.

effect: Intronic variant in linkage disequilibrium with promoter variant affecting warfarin sensitivity
metabolizer_impact: In high LD with rs9923231. Used as alternative genotyping marker for VKORC1 haplotype in warfarin dosing algorithms.
clinical_significance: Part of the VKORC1 haplotype that determines warfarin dose requirement. Included in several pharmacogenomic dosing algorithms.
description: Intronic variant (1173C>T) used as a tag SNP for the VKORC1 low-expression haplotype.

effect: Missense variant affecting P-glycoprotein function
metabolizer_impact: T allele: altered P-gp function. May affect drug absorption and distribution. Often analyzed with rs1045642 as ABCB1 haplotype.
clinical_significance: Common triallelic variant (G/T/A). Effect on drug disposition depends on haplotype context with other ABCB1 variants.
description: Ser893Ala/Thr (G2677T/A) missense variant affecting P-glycoprotein substrate specificity and transport activity.

effect: Common polymorphism — normal DPD function
metabolizer_impact: Normal DPD activity. No dose adjustment required for fluoropyrimidines.
clinical_significance: Ile543Val variant. Previously thought to affect DPD activity but now classified as normal function. Included in some panels.
description: Synonymous variant commonly genotyped in DPYD panels. Does not affect enzyme function.

effect: Non-functional — complete loss of DPD enzyme activity from this allele
metabolizer_impact: See DPYD *2A entry above.
clinical_significance: Duplicate entry consolidated — see primary DPYD *2A entry.
description: IVS14+1G>A splice site variant. See primary entry.

effect: Decreased function — reduced DPD activity through aberrant splicing
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous with normal allele: activity score 1.5. Dose reduction of 25-50% recommended for fluoropyrimidines.
clinical_significance: Found in ~2-4% of Caucasians. Fourth key DPYD variant recommended for clinical testing alongside *2A, *13, and c.2846A>T.
description: Intronic variant in linkage disequilibrium with c.1129-5923C>G, causing partial skipping of exon 11 and reduced DPD activity.

effect: Decreased function — reduced UGT1A1 expression
metabolizer_impact: In strong LD with *28. Can be used as surrogate marker for *28 in genotyping platforms that cannot detect TA repeats.
clinical_significance: Tag SNP for UGT1A1*28. T allele correlates with (TA)7 repeat (*28). Useful for array-based pharmacogenomic testing.
description: Promoter variant (c.-364C>T) in linkage disequilibrium with the *28 TA repeat polymorphism.

effect: Increased function — enhanced hepatic uptake
metabolizer_impact: Increased OATP1B1 function. May lower systemic statin exposure. When combined with *5 (as *15 haplotype), effect depends on specific combination.
clinical_significance: Common globally. The *1B allele alone enhances hepatic uptake. Part of the *15 haplotype (*1B + *5) common in East Asians.
description: Promoter variant (-11187G>A) increasing SLCO1B1 transcription.

effect: Increased function — enhanced transporter activity
metabolizer_impact: Asn130Asp variant associated with increased OATP1B1 function. Part of the *1B haplotype.
clinical_significance: Common globally (~40% allele frequency). Contributes to normal/increased hepatic drug uptake.
description: Asn130Asp (c.388A>G) substitution enhancing OATP1B1 transport activity.

effect: Reduced statin response — attenuated LDL lowering
metabolizer_impact: A allele carriers may have attenuated LDL-cholesterol response to statin therapy (~20% less reduction).
clinical_significance: Found in ~5% of Caucasians. May explain some of the variability in statin response. Patients may need higher doses or combination therapy.
description: Variant in HMG-CoA reductase gene affecting statin target. May reduce statin binding affinity or enzyme regulation.

effect: Increased warfarin sensitivity in African Americans
metabolizer_impact: G allele carriers (common in African Americans) require lower warfarin doses (~7% per allele). Important for dosing algorithms in diverse populations.
clinical_significance: The most significant pharmacogenomic variant for warfarin dosing in African Americans, beyond CYP2C9 and VKORC1. Improves dosing algorithm accuracy.
description: Intergenic variant near CYP2C gene cluster associated with reduced warfarin clearance through unknown mechanism. Important for pharmacogenomic equity.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced CYP3A4 activity
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP3A4 metabolism. May increase drug exposure for CYP3A4 substrates.
clinical_significance: Rare allele (~1-2% frequency). Clinical impact less well-established than CYP3A5 variants for most drugs.
description: Met445Thr substitution reducing CYP3A4 catalytic activity.

effect: Decreased function allele — reduced CYP3A4 expression
metabolizer_impact: ~30-50% reduced CYP3A4 expression. Carriers may need lower doses of CYP3A4 substrates. Particularly relevant for tacrolimus dosing.
clinical_significance: Found in ~5-8% of Caucasians. Increasingly recognized as important for tacrolimus dosing alongside CYP3A5 genotype.
description: Intronic variant (15389C>T) causing alternative splicing and reduced CYP3A4 mRNA levels.

effect: Possible increased function — enhanced promoter activity
metabolizer_impact: A allele may increase CYP3A4 expression. Clinical significance debated — may contribute to variability in tacrolimus requirements.
clinical_significance: Common in African populations (~60-80%). Rare in Caucasians and East Asians. Effect on drug metabolism remains under investigation.
description: Promoter variant (-392A>G) with variable effect on CYP3A4 transcription.

effect: Non-functional allele — frameshift deletion abolishing enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: No CYP2C9 activity from this allele. Same clinical impact as *3 homozygosity when combined with another loss-of-function allele.
clinical_significance: Rare allele found primarily in African-American populations (~1% allele frequency). Important for comprehensive CYP2C9 genotyping in diverse populations.
description: Single base deletion (818delA) in exon 5 causing frameshift and premature termination.

effect: Decreased function — reduced vitamin K1 metabolism
metabolizer_impact: TT genotype: reduced vitamin K1 hydroxylation. Higher vitamin K1 levels in liver require ~1mg/day higher warfarin dose. Adds to CYP2C9/VKORC1 dosing algorithms.
clinical_significance: Found in ~20-30% of Caucasians. Accounts for ~1-2% of warfarin dose variability. Included in refined pharmacogenomic dosing algorithms.
description: Val433Met substitution reducing CYP4F2 metabolism of vitamin K1 oxidase activity.

effect: Non-functional allele — reduced enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Contributes to poor metabolizer status for CYP2C19 substrates.
clinical_significance: Rare variant included in expanded CYP2C19 genotyping panels.
description: Variant defining the *4B haplotype with reduced CYP2C19 activity.

effect: Non-functional allele — premature stop codon
metabolizer_impact: No enzyme activity. Same clinical impact as *2 when combined with other loss-of-function alleles.
clinical_significance: Rare allele (~0.1-0.5% globally). Important for comprehensive CYP2C19 genotyping.
description: Premature stop codon (A>G at position 1) in exon 1 of CYP2C19.

effect: Non-functional allele — mutation in start codon region
metabolizer_impact: No functional CYP2C19 enzyme production.
clinical_significance: Very rare variant. Included in comprehensive CYP2C19 genotyping panels.
description: Variant affecting CYP2C19 translation initiation.

effect: Non-functional allele — missense mutation abolishing activity
metabolizer_impact: No functional CYP2C19 enzyme activity.
clinical_significance: Very rare. Included in extended CYP2C19 panels.
description: Arg132Gln substitution abolishing CYP2C19 enzymatic activity.

effect: Non-functional allele
metabolizer_impact: No functional CYP2C19 enzyme activity. Poor metabolizer when combined with another non-functional allele.
clinical_significance: Rare variant. Part of comprehensive CYP2C19 genotyping.
description: Trp120Arg substitution abolishing enzyme activity.

effect: Decreased function allele
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2C9 activity. May require warfarin dose adjustment.
clinical_significance: Found primarily in Middle Eastern and African populations.
description: Arg125His substitution reducing CYP2C9 catalytic activity.

effect: Warfarin resistance variant — increased VKORC1 expression
metabolizer_impact: Contributes to warfarin dose requirement variability. Part of VKORC1 haplotype analysis.
clinical_significance: Used in some warfarin dosing algorithms as part of VKORC1 haplotype determination.
description: 3'UTR variant affecting VKORC1 mRNA stability.

effect: Decreased function — reduced electron transfer to CYP enzymes
metabolizer_impact: Reduced P450 oxidoreductase activity affecting multiple CYP enzymes. May modestly alter metabolism of various drugs.
clinical_significance: Found in ~25-30% of Caucasians. Modulates activity of multiple CYP enzymes. Effect on individual drugs is typically modest.
description: Ala503Val substitution reducing electron transfer from POR to CYP enzymes, modestly affecting activity of CYP1A2, CYP2C19, CYP3A4.

effect: Part of *3A haplotype when combined with *3C (rs1142345)
metabolizer_impact: See TPMT *3B entry. Combined *3B+*3C = *3A, the most common TPMT non-functional allele in Caucasians.
clinical_significance: *3A accounts for ~5% of TPMT alleles in Caucasians. ~0.3% are homozygous or compound heterozygous PM.
description: Part of the *3A haplotype. When both Ala154Thr (*3B) and Tyr240Cys (*3C) are present on the same chromosome, defines TPMT*3A.

effect: Non-functional allele — loss of NUDT15 enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Heterozygous: intermediate metabolizer — significant dose reduction (50-75%) needed. Homozygous: poor metabolizer — thiopurines severely toxic at standard doses.
clinical_significance: Critical in East Asian and Hispanic populations: ~10-20% carry at least one *3 allele. Explains thiopurine intolerance in TPMT-normal Asian patients. CPIC now recommends combined TPMT + NUDT15 testing.
description: Arg139Cys substitution abolishing NUDT15 dephosphorylation activity, leading to excess accumulation of thioguanine nucleotides and severe myelosuppression.

effect: Decreased function allele
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2B6 activity. May increase efavirenz plasma levels.
clinical_significance: Found in ~10-15% of Caucasians. Contributes to slow metabolizer phenotype for CYP2B6 substrates.
description: Arg487Cys substitution reducing CYP2B6 catalytic activity.

effect: Non-functional allele — severely impaired enzyme activity
metabolizer_impact: Dramatically reduced CYP2B6 activity. High efavirenz plasma levels with increased CNS toxicity risk.
clinical_significance: Found almost exclusively in African populations (~4-8%). Important for efavirenz dosing in sub-Saharan Africa. Supports reduced 400mg dose.
description: Ile328Thr substitution severely reducing CYP2B6 protein stability and catalytic function.

effect: Altered reduced folate carrier function
metabolizer_impact: G/G genotype: reduced methotrexate uptake into cells. May affect methotrexate efficacy in rheumatoid arthritis and some cancers.
clinical_significance: Common variant (~50% allele frequency). Clinical relevance most established for methotrexate in rheumatoid arthritis.
description: His27Arg (G80A) substitution in the reduced folate carrier affecting methotrexate transport into cells.

effect: Reduced MTHFR activity affecting folate metabolism and methotrexate response
metabolizer_impact: Combined with rs1801133, affects methotrexate toxicity risk through altered folate metabolism.
clinical_significance: May contribute to methotrexate toxicity risk. Folate supplementation recommended during methotrexate therapy regardless of genotype.
description: MTHFR A1298C variant affecting enzyme activity and folate-dependent drug metabolism.

effect: Altered pregnane X receptor expression affecting CYP3A4 induction
metabolizer_impact: TT genotype: reduced PXR expression — less CYP3A4 induction. May affect drug interactions involving enzyme induction.
clinical_significance: Modulates the extent of CYP3A4 induction by drugs and dietary compounds. May explain some inter-individual variability in drug-drug interactions.
description: Promoter variant (-25385C>T) reducing NR1I2/PXR transcription and downstream CYP3A4 inducibility.

effect: Increased CYP1A2 expression affecting caffeine and drug metabolism
metabolizer_impact: T allele: increased CYP1A2 activity. Faster caffeine metabolism. May need higher doses of CYP1A2-metabolized drugs.
clinical_significance: Common variant associated with coffee consumption habits. Relevant for clozapine and theophylline dosing.
description: Intergenic variant near CYP1A1/CYP1A2 associated with increased CYP1A2 expression and higher caffeine consumption.

effect: Critical for clopidogrel activation — see primary entry
metabolizer_impact: FDA black box warning: CYP2C19 PMs have reduced clopidogrel activation. Use prasugrel or ticagrelor instead.
clinical_significance: FDA-mandated pharmacogenomic information in clopidogrel labeling since 2010.
description: See primary CYP2C19 *2 entry. Duplicated for clopidogrel-specific guidance.

effect: Reduced MTHFR activity affecting methotrexate response
metabolizer_impact: TT genotype: increased methotrexate toxicity risk due to impaired folate metabolism. Folate supplementation critical.
clinical_significance: High folate supplementation recommended during methotrexate therapy for TT carriers. Also affects 5-FU pharmacodynamics.
description: MTHFR C677T affecting folate pathway and chemotherapy response.

effect: Altered dopamine D2 receptor expression affecting antipsychotic response
metabolizer_impact: T allele: reduced D2 receptor expression. May affect antipsychotic efficacy and side effect profile.
clinical_significance: Emerging pharmacogenomic marker for antipsychotic response. Currently research-level evidence.
description: Synonymous variant (C957T) affecting DRD2 mRNA stability and D2 receptor density.

effect: Increased risk of antipsychotic-induced weight gain
metabolizer_impact: A allele carriers: significantly increased risk of weight gain with second-generation antipsychotics (~2-3x risk).
clinical_significance: MC4R plays a central role in appetite regulation. This variant may help predict which patients will experience metabolic side effects from antipsychotics.
description: Near-gene variant affecting melanocortin-4 receptor regulation and antipsychotic-induced metabolic effects.

effect: Altered catecholamine metabolism affecting opioid dose requirements
metabolizer_impact: Low COMT activity (Met/Met at Val158Met): may require lower opioid doses due to higher pain sensitivity but increased opioid effect. High COMT: may need higher doses.
clinical_significance: Emerging evidence for COMT genotype-guided opioid dosing. Currently used primarily in research settings.
description: Variant in linkage disequilibrium with Val158Met (rs4680) affecting catecholamine and endorphin metabolism.

effect: Altered P-glycoprotein function
metabolizer_impact: May affect opioid brain penetration and response. Part of ABCB1 haplotype analysis.
clinical_significance: Intronic variant contributing to ABCB1 functional haplotypes.
description: Intronic variant in ABCB1 gene contributing to P-glycoprotein expression variability.

effect: Decreased function allele in African populations
metabolizer_impact: See CYP2C9 *8 entry above (rs1058930).
clinical_significance: Part of the same *8 haplotype. Important for warfarin pharmacogenomics in African-descent populations.
description: Additional variant defining CYP2C9 *8 haplotype.

effect: Slow acetylator allele — reduced NAT2 activity
metabolizer_impact: Contributes to slow acetylator phenotype when combined with other slow alleles.
clinical_significance: Part of NAT2 acetylator phenotype determination. ~50% of Caucasians are slow acetylators.
description: Synonymous variant (C282T) used as part of NAT2 haplotype determination for acetylator phenotype.

effect: Slow acetylator allele — reduced NAT2 enzyme stability
metabolizer_impact: Slow acetylator. Same clinical implications as other slow NAT2 alleles.
clinical_significance: Relatively common (~2-5% allele frequency in Caucasians). More common in some Asian populations.
description: Gly286Glu substitution (G857A) reducing NAT2 protein stability and acetylation capacity.

effect: Decreased function — reduced CYP2D6 expression
metabolizer_impact: See primary CYP2D6 *41 entry. Important for opioid and tamoxifen metabolism.
clinical_significance: Particularly important in populations where *41 is common (Middle Eastern, North African).
description: See primary entry. CYP2D6*41 with reduced mRNA expression.

effect: Synonymous variant affecting P-glycoprotein expression
metabolizer_impact: T allele: may reduce P-gp expression. Part of ABCB1 haplotype analyzed with rs2032582 and rs1045642.
clinical_significance: Clinical effect depends on haplotype context with other ABCB1 variants.
description: Synonymous variant (1236C>T, Gly412Gly) affecting mRNA folding and P-glycoprotein expression.

effect: Part of VKORC1 haplotype affecting warfarin sensitivity
metabolizer_impact: Contributes to VKORC1 haplotype determination. In LD with rs9923231.
clinical_significance: Used in some pharmacogenomic platforms as part of VKORC1 haplotype analysis.
description: Intronic variant used for VKORC1 haplotype assignment.

effect: Altered OATP1B3 transporter function
metabolizer_impact: T allele: reduced OATP1B3 transport activity. May increase systemic exposure to substrate drugs.
clinical_significance: Emerging pharmacogenomic marker. Clinical implementation still developing for most substrate drugs.
description: Ser112Ala substitution affecting OATP1B3 substrate transport.

effect: Reduced MRP2 transporter expression
metabolizer_impact: T allele: reduced MRP2 expression. May increase methotrexate toxicity through reduced biliary excretion.
clinical_significance: Found in ~15-20% of Caucasians. May contribute to variability in methotrexate toxicity and clearance.
description: Promoter variant (-24C>T) reducing ABCC2/MRP2 transcription and biliary drug excretion.

effect: Decreased function allele
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP3A4 activity. May increase drug exposure.
clinical_significance: Rare variant. Primarily found in specific populations.
description: Phe189Ser substitution reducing CYP3A4 catalytic activity.

effect: Part of CYP2C19 haplotype structure
metabolizer_impact: Used in CYP2C19 haplotype phasing and metabolizer status determination.
clinical_significance: Part of comprehensive CYP2C19 genotyping panels.
description: Intronic variant used for CYP2C19 haplotype assignment.

effect: Tag SNP for CYP2C19 haplotypes
metabolizer_impact: Used in combination with other SNPs for accurate CYP2C19 metabolizer status prediction.
clinical_significance: Part of expanded CYP2C19 genotyping panels.
description: Intronic variant contributing to CYP2C19 haplotype determination.

effect: Shared variant between CYP2C9 and CYP2C19 haplotypes
metabolizer_impact: Part of CYP2C gene cluster haplotype analysis.
clinical_significance: Used in haplotype phasing for CYP2C gene cluster pharmacogenomics.
description: Variant in CYP2C gene cluster used for haplotype determination.

effect: Part of CYP2D6 haplotype structure
metabolizer_impact: Used in CYP2D6 haplotype phasing for metabolizer status determination.
clinical_significance: Included in expanded CYP2D6 genotyping panels.
description: Intronic variant used for CYP2D6 haplotype assignment.

effect: Part of CYP2D6 *2 and *10 haplotypes
metabolizer_impact: Ser486Thr substitution. Part of *2 (normal) and other CYP2D6 haplotypes.
clinical_significance: Used for accurate CYP2D6 star allele assignment.
description: Ser486Thr (C>G at position 4180) variant used in CYP2D6 haplotype determination.

effect: Decreased function — reduced glucuronidation activity
metabolizer_impact: *2/*2: reduced glucuronidation. May increase lorazepam and oxazepam exposure.
clinical_significance: Common variant. Clinical significance emerging for benzodiazepine metabolism.
description: Asp85Tyr substitution reducing UGT2B15 glucuronidation activity.

effect: Decreased function — reduced DPD expression
metabolizer_impact: Reduced DPD activity. Dose modification may be needed for fluoropyrimidines.
clinical_significance: Rare variant with evidence of reduced DPD function.
description: Promoter variant affecting DPYD transcription.

effect: Possible decreased function — clinical significance uncertain
metabolizer_impact: Met166Val variant. Evidence for reduced DPD activity is inconsistent. Not currently used for clinical dose adjustment.
clinical_significance: Included in some DPYD panels but clinical actionability is uncertain. More evidence needed.
description: Met166Val substitution with uncertain effect on DPD enzyme activity.

effect: Abacavir hypersensitivity — immune-mediated reaction
metabolizer_impact: CONTRAINDICATED. 48% positive predictive value for hypersensitivity. 100% negative predictive value — safe to use if HLA-B*57:01 negative.
clinical_significance: Most successful pharmacogenomic implementation in clinical practice. Mandatory testing has virtually eliminated abacavir hypersensitivity reactions.
description: See primary HLA-B*57:01 entry. Most impactful pharmacogenomic test in HIV medicine.

effect: Normal function variant — not associated with G6PD deficiency
metabolizer_impact: Normal G6PD activity. Standard drug use. Note: G6PD deficiency testing should combine multiple variants.
clinical_significance: Asn126Asp variant defining the A+ allele (normal activity). When combined with Val68Met (rs1050828), defines the A- deficiency allele.
description: Asn126Asp substitution (A376G). Alone (A+ allele) has normal enzyme activity; combined with Val68Met creates deficient A- allele.

effect: Decreased function allele
metabolizer_impact: Reduced CYP2C9 activity. May require warfarin dose reduction.
clinical_significance: Found in ~2% of African Americans. Contributes to warfarin dose variability in diverse populations.
description: Arg150Leu substitution reducing CYP2C9 activity.

effect: Most impactful CYP2C9 variant for warfarin dosing
metabolizer_impact: See primary CYP2C9*3 entry. *3/*3 requires ~70% warfarin dose reduction.
clinical_significance: Combined CYP2C9+VKORC1 genotyping accounts for ~40-50% of warfarin dose variability.
description: See primary entry. Ile359Leu with severe impact on warfarin metabolism.
